Glossary About
The Greek beginnings · 5th c. BCE – 3rd c. CE

Antiquity

The ancient Mediterranean is where soul (ψυχή) becomes the central object of philosophical inquiry. From Plato and Aristotle to Stoic materialism and Lucretian atomism, the Greeks and Romans define almost every vocabulary later philosophy of mind will borrow.

c. 428 – c. 348 BCE

Plato

The philosopher in whom soul becomes the central object of care, inquiry, and rule.

384 – 322 BCE

Aristotle

Soul as the form of a living body: hylomorphism, faculties, and the beginnings of a scientific psychology.

3rd c. BCE – 2nd c. CE

The Stoics

A materialist psychology of assent, impulse, and disciplined inner life.

1st c. BCE – 2nd c. CE

Before Plotinus

Middle Platonism, Philo, and the Peripatetic retrieval that sets the stage for Neoplatonism.

c. 99 – c. 55 BCE

Lucretius

Atomism, mortal soul, and the therapy of fear through nature.

Digital Minds · A history of thinking about the mind, from antiquity to the present day.
A personal research project by Fabián. · About · Glossary
